full_prompt: Design a small two-story bolted steel-framed building per KDS 41 and KDS 14 30 25. The building footprint is 6 m x 8 m with story height 3.5 m per floor (total 7.0 m). The frame consists of 4 columns and orthogonal beams at each floor. Column sections are H-200x200x8/12 (representative wide-flange per KS D 3503), beam sections H-400x200x8/13. The beam-to-column connections are bolted moment connections using M20 high-strength bolts, grade F10T (equivalent to ASTM A490, min tensile 1040 MPa, min yield 900 MPa), with bolts in double-nut configuration per KDS 14 30 25 requirements for slip-critical joints. Loads: dead load 5.0 kPa on floor slab, live load 2.5 kPa (residential per KDS 41), wind load 1.2 kPa (seismic zone 1 reference), snow load 0.5 kPa (urban reference, KDS 41 wind/snow map). Seismic load: design spectral acceleration SDS = 0.5 g, SD1 = 0.2 g (urban equivalent). Load combinations per KDS 41 chapter 5 (LRFD): 1.4D; 1.2D + 1.6L; 1.2D + 1.0L + 1.3W; 1.2D + 1.0L + 1.0E; the governing combo is 1.2D + 1.0L + 1.0E for lateral. Steel is SM355 (equivalent to ASTM A572 Gr 50, yield 355 MPa, ultimate 490 MPa). Pass/fail criteria per KDS 14 30 25: (1) no member stress may exceed the LRFD factored resistance (phi * Fy for tension, phi * nominal shear for connections, phi = 0.9 for tension, 0.75 for shear rupture at connections), (2) maximum inter-story drift under lateral seismic must not exceed H/200 = 17.5 mm, (3) bolt shear utilization at worst-case connection must not exceed 1.0 (demand/capacity per KDS 14 30 25 eqns), (4) floor deflection under 1.0D + 1.0L must not exceed L/360 = 22.2 mm at an 8 m beam. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA (beam elements for frame, full-solid or shell for the connection detail) plus an LRFD code check using KDS 14 30 25 formulas (non-FEA code calculation). Seismic response can be approximated by equivalent-lateral-force per KDS 41 chapter 6; full response-history analysis is optional. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at this design gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the bare frame + one representative connection detail, a code-check spreadsheet, and a report listing max column stress utilization, max beam deflection in mm, max inter-story drift in mm, and bolt utilization at worst connection.
short_prompt:
Design a two-story bolted steel-framed building per KDS 41 and KDS 14 30 25. Footprint 6 x 8 m with 3.5 m story height (2 stories, total 7.0 m). Four columns in H-200x200x8/12 and orthogonal beams in H-400x200x8/13 (KS D 3503 wide-flange). Steel is SM355 (Fy = 355 MPa, Fu = 490 MPa). Beam-to-column connections are bolted moment connections using M20 F10T high-strength bolts (slip-critical, double-nut). Column bases fixed. LRFD checks per KDS 14 30 25 (phi = 0.9 tension, 0.75 shear rupture).

Characteristic loads: D = 5.0 kPa, L = 2.5 kPa (residential), W = 1.2 kPa, S = 0.5 kPa, seismic SDS = 0.5 g / SD1 = 0.2 g.

Load cases (KDS 41 LRFD combinations).
- 1.4 D.
- 1.2 D + 1.6 L.
- 1.2 D + 1.0 L + 1.3 W.
- 1.2 D + 1.0 L + 1.0 E (governing lateral).
Serviceability: L/360 beam deflection (22.2 mm at 8 m), H/200 inter-story drift (17.5 mm at 3.5 m story).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: KDS 14 30 25 capacity formulas (phi*Fy etc.) are analytical closed-form checks next to the FEA output.

prompt.title: KDS 41 + KDS 14 30 25 small steel framed building
prompt.brief: Design a 6 x 8 m, 2-story bolted steel frame per KDS 41 + KDS 14 30 25 with representative SM355 sections and F10T bolts.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: KDS 14 30 25 bolt shear capacity with phi=0.75
